title: Methods and Return Types
description:
Methods in Java must declare their return type. A method that returns nothing uses 'void', otherwise specify the type.

Fix the method signatures and return statements.
objectives: Add correct return types to methods
Ensure return statements match the declared type
hints: A method returning an int needs 'public static int methodName()'.
Methods with no return value use 'void' and don't need a return statement.

checks.failMessage: The add method should return int.
The greet method should return void.
The isEven method should return boolean.
